title: helianthemum glaucum subsp. suffruticosum
intro: Helianthemum glaucum subsp. suffruticosum, commonly known as rockrose, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of rockrose have been found to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, providing various health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, reduced oxidative stress, and enhanced immune function. Rockrose can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat minor skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues.
